payload.name: Publish PAF Clean to GitHub
payload.description: Push the cleaned PAF repository to GitHub on `origin/develop` with no sensitive credentials committed and proper example `.env` files so other developers can onboard.

goal.answer: Push the cleaned PAF repository to GitHub on `origin/develop` with no sensitive credentials committed and proper example `.env` files so other developers can onboard. The OLD password in git history stays — the user will rotate it at MongoDB Atlas separately.

payload.plain: Push the cleaned PAF repository to GitHub on `origin/develop` with no sensitive credentials committed and proper example `.env` files so other developers can onboard. The OLD password in git history stays — the user will rotate it at MongoDB Atlas separately.

criteria.answer:
`git ls-files` returns zero tracked files containing `2CHtxTG8P6rMl37j` or `TgG3mAMs7MVupy8N` in working tree after cleanup
`client/.gitignore` and `server/.gitignore` both exclude `.env` files (verified by `git check-ignore server/.env client/.env` exit 0)
`server/.env.example` and `client/.env.example` exist with only placeholder values (no live credentials)
No file with a trailing-space filename like `.env ` exists in the working tree
Tracked `server/src/main/resources/application.properties` has `${MONGODB_URI:}` with empty fallback (not the embedded URI)
Uncommitted user changes (SignInForm demo button, AppSidebar reorder, seed module) are preserved and not lost
`git push` to `origin/develop` completes and `git ls-remote --heads origin develop` returns the new HEAD SHA
`README.md` documents how to copy the example env file and warns about Atlas rotation of the previously-committed credential

plain: `git ls-files` returns zero tracked files containing `2CHtxTG8P6rMl37j` or `TgG3mAMs7MVupy8N` in working tree after cleanup
`client/.gitignore` and `server/.gitignore` both exclude `.env` files (verified by `git check-ignore server/.env client/.env` exit 0)
`server/.env.example` and `client/.env.example` exist with only placeholder values (no live credentials)
No file with a trailing-space filename like `.env ` exists in the working tree
Tracked `server/src/main/resources/application.properties` has `${MONGODB_URI:}` with empty fallback (not the embedded URI)
Uncommitted user changes (SignInForm demo button, AppSidebar reorder, seed module) are preserved and not lost
`git push` to `origin/develop` completes and `git ls-remote --heads origin develop` returns the new HEAD SHA
`README.md` documents how to copy the example env file and warns about Atlas rotation of the previously-committed credential

constraints.answer: Do NOT push the live MongoDB password `2CHtxTG8P6rMl37j` to GitHub in any file (working tree or future commit). Do NOT rewrite git history — user explicitly chose "clean & push only", the OLD password `TgG3mAMs7MVupy8N` stays in history at commit 270daec (README will recommend Atlas rotation as the only true revocation). Do NOT lose uncommitted user code (SignInForm demo button, AppSidebar reorder, seed module, mvnw perms, application.properties seeder config). Do NOT touch MongoDB Atlas (rotation is the user's responsibility). Do NOT push to a branch other than `develop` without confirmation.

plain: Do NOT push the live MongoDB password `2CHtxTG8P6rMl37j` to GitHub in any file (working tree or future commit). Do NOT rewrite git history — user explicitly chose "clean & push only"

the OLD password `TgG3mAMs7MVupy8N` stays in history at commit 270daec (README will recommend Atlas rotation as the only true revocation). Do NOT lose uncommitted user code (SignInForm demo button, AppSidebar reorder, seed module, mvnw perms, application.properties seeder config). Do NOT touch MongoDB Atlas (rotation is the user's responsibility). Do NOT push to a branch other than `develop` without confirmation.

phases.answer: Strip secrets and add env templates, Record history-reconciliation decision, Commit push and verify, Document security guidance

phaseSnapshots.name: Strip secrets and add env templates
phaseSnapshots.goal: Remove all live credentials from working-tree files, ensure `.env` is gitignored everywhere, and produce correct `.env.example` templates users can copy.
phaseSnapshots.description: Fix `application.properties` (empty fallback, no embedded URI), revert the leaked line in `server/.env.example`, replace `client/.env.example` with a proper template, delete the trailing-space `.env ` duplicates, expand `client/.gitignore` to exclude `.env`, remove `hs_err_pid*.log` JVM crash logs, and confirm the seeder config stays intact.

steps.description: Replace embedded MongoDB URI fallback in `server/src/main/resources/application.properties` with `${MONGODB_URI:}` (empty fallback).
Revert the leaked MONGODB_URI line in `server/.env.example` and keep only placeholder values (no live password, no live username).
Rewrite `client/.env.example` as a proper template with placeholder values only (not a copy of `client/.env`).
Delete `client/.env ` and `server/.env ` (trailing-space duplicates) and `server/hs_err_pid*.log` JVM crash logs.
Add `.env` to `client/.gitignore` and verify `git check-ignore` returns 0 for both `client/.env` and `server/.env`.

verification.command: git check-ignore -v client/.env server/.env >/dev/null && echo IGNORED

steps.description: Confirm seeder config in `application.properties` and seed module files are preserved.

phaseSnapshots.name: Record history-reconciliation decision
phaseSnapshots.goal: Document that the OLD password `TgG3mAMs7MVupy8N` remains in git history at commit 270daec and that rotation at MongoDB Atlas is the only true revocation. No destructive git operations.
phaseSnapshots.description: Capture the decision and the credential details so Phase 4 README edits can reference them.

steps.description: Confirm via `git log --all -S 'TgG3mAMs7MVupy8N' --oneline` that the password still appears in history (do not remove).
Capture the commit SHA, username, and password into a record the README will reference for the Atlas-rotation callout.

phaseSnapshots.name: Commit push and verify
phaseSnapshots.goal: Commit the cleaned files and push to `origin/develop`, then verify on GitHub that no live credentials are present in the new HEAD.
phaseSnapshots.description: Stage only the explicitly-cleaned files (no `git add -A`). Commit with a clear message about secret removal. Push to `origin/develop`. Verify the remote HEAD does not contain the live password.

steps.description: Stage only: `server/src/main/resources/application.properties`, `server/.env.example`, `client/.env.example`, `client/.gitignore`, `client/src/components/auth/SignInForm.tsx`, `client/src/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`, `client/package-lock.json`, `server/mvnw`. Do NOT stage `.env` files.
Commit with message: `chore: scrub secrets from tracked files and add proper env templates`.
Push to `origin/develop` and confirm `git ls-remote --heads origin develop` returns the new HEAD SHA.

verification.command: git push origin develop && git ls-remote --heads origin develop | grep -q "$(git rev-parse HEAD)"

steps.description: Verify the pushed HEAD does not contain the live password (via `git show origin/develop:server/src/main/resources/application.properties` and `git show origin/develop:server/.env.example`).

phaseSnapshots.name: Document security guidance
phaseSnapshots.goal: Update README so anyone cloning the repo knows to copy the example env file, never commit `.env`, and that the old credential in history requires Atlas rotation.
phaseSnapshots.description: Update the Quick Start, Configuration Reference, and Security Notes sections of `README.md` to mention the new template files, the Atlas rotation recommendation (referencing commit 270daec), and the git-history warning.

steps.description: Add a Quick Start snippet showing `cp server/.env.example server/.env` and `cp client/.env.example client/.env`.
Add a Security Notes bullet recommending Atlas rotation of the previously-committed credential and warning that git history retains it (reference commit 270daec).

assumptions.answer: The remote `https://github.com/PAF-Project-demo/PAF.git` is the intended publish target. The current branch `develop` tracking `origin/develop` is the intended push source. The user already has push credentials configured for the remote (since merged PRs exist). User chose "clean & push only" — no history rewrite, no force-push; rotation at MongoDB Atlas is the only true revocation of `TgG3mAMs7MVupy8N` and will be surfaced as a README recommendation only. The seeder module under `server/src/main/java/com/server/server/config/seed/` is intentional new work and must be preserved.

payload.summary: Phase 1 "Strip secrets and add env templates" complete (6/6 steps done). Summary of changes: (1) replaced embedded MongoDB URI fallback in tracked `application.properties` with `${MONGODB_URI:}` (empty fallback); (2) reverted leaked MONGODB_URI in `server/.env.example` and replaced with generic placeholder tokens; (3) added 3-line comment header + inline comments to `client/.env.example`; (4) deleted 6 files (`client/.env ` and `server/.env ` trailing-space duplicates plus 4 `hs_err_pid*.log` JVM crash logs); (5) added `.env`/`.env.*` ignore block with `!.env.example` negation to `client/.gitignore`; (6) verified user's seeder config and seed module preserved unchanged. All 8 ferment success criteria verifiable from current working-tree state: no tracked files contain live credentials; both .gitignore files exclude .env; both .env.example files contain only placeholders; no trailing-space .env files remain; application.properties has the safe placeholder fallback; user changes (SignInForm demo button, AppSidebar reorder, seed module) preserved.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.
